chat.title: Bitchat — Install Guide
chat.guide_subtitle: Install Bitchat on every team phone for offline mesh communication when cellular networks are down.
chat.why_body: Bitchat is a free, open-source messenger that uses Bluetooth mesh networking — no internet, no SIM card, no servers. Messages hop through nearby phones (up to 7 hops) to reach distant team members. Designed for crisis zones where cellular networks fail. Browser-based access is limited; field teams should install the native apps below.
chat.no_apk_yet: No APK uploaded yet. An organisation admin can upload one in Settings while online; field teams can then download it offline. For now:
chat.test_step1: Two team members install Bitchat on their phones.
chat.test_step2: Both pick the same channel — for example #test.
chat.test_step3: From within ~30 m line-of-sight (longer with hops), one sends "hello".
chat.test_step4: Confirm the other receives it. If yes, the mesh is working — schedule training.
chat.org_rec1: Use sector-based channel names matching your AidFlow Pro sectors (e.g. #sector-b-north, #medical-team).
chat.org_rec2: Standardise nicknames as ROLE-NAME (FW-Pierre for field worker, MED-Carmen for medical, ADM-Sarah for admin).
chat.org_rec3: Brief everyone on Bitchat's privacy model: messages are end-to-end encrypted but sent in cleartext on each hop is impossible — Bitchat uses Noise Protocol XX so peers can't read each other's traffic.
chat.org_rec4: If a phone is lost, it can't be remotely wiped — keep tactical info in chat short and time-bounded.

android.step1_body: Tap the download button at the top of this page (if your admin uploaded an APK), or get it from
android.step2_body: Open Settings → Apps → Special access → Install unknown apps, find your browser, and toggle "Allow from this source".
android.step3_body: Open your Downloads folder, tap bitchat.apk, then tap Install. Android will warn you the app isn't from Play Store — that's expected. Approve.
android.step4_body: On first launch, Bitchat asks for Bluetooth and Location permissions. Both are required for BLE mesh discovery. Tap Allow.
android.step5_body: Pick a short nickname matching your role (e.g. FW-Pierre or MED-Carmen). Don't use real names if you're operating in a sensitive area.
android.step6_body: Tap the # icon to add channels matching what your AidFlow Pro org uses. You're now ready to send messages — they'll relay through any nearby Bitchat user.

chat.settings_help: Upload an .apk file once while online. Field teams connecting to this AidFlow instance can then download Bitchat offline. Get the latest APK from {{url}}.
